materials: Hook matching your chosen yarn weight (suggest 3.5mm/E for worsted yarn)
Optional: clear nail polish or white glue

rounds.rawInstruction: with green, make a slip knot and ch 14; sl st in the first ch you made, to join the piece in a circle; [ch 3, sl st in the next ch of the circle] 14 times (work your last sl st into the st that joined the circle)
rounds.summary: Create a foundation chain and join into a circle, then make 14 chain loops around it.
rounds.title: Rnd 1

rounds.rawInstruction: Fasten off and weave in the ends (or keep them for sewing, if the wreath will become an applique).
rounds.summary: Fasten off the green yarn and weave in the ends.
rounds.title: Fasten off and weave in the ends

rounds.rawInstruction: Take a 30-40cm/12-16" length of red yarn, thread it into a yarn needle and weave it through the chain loops around the wreath, starting and ending at the join. Tie the ends in a bow, then cut them to the length you like.
rounds.summary: Weave red yarn through the chain loops to create a bow.
rounds.title: Add red yarn bow

rounds.rawInstruction: Optionally, if your yarn holds knots poorly or tends to fray at the ends, dot some clear nail polish or white glue at the knot and at the ends, to secure them.
rounds.summary: Optionally secure the bow knot and ends with clear nail polish or glue.
rounds.title: Optionally secure knot and ends
